title: cestrum aurantiacum f. regelii
intro: Cestrum aurantiacum f. regelii, also known as the yellow cestrum or night-blooming jessamine, is a flowering plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its bright yellow, fragrant flowers that bloom at night. The plant contains a number of medicinal compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Cestrum aurantiacum f. regelii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
